title: Fabricia hamosa
intro: Fabricia hamosa, also known as Fabricia, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in the wild in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Fabricia hamosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Fabricia hamosa is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Fabricia hamosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Australia and is known to be an annual herb. The exact classification of Fabricia hamosa can be further specified as follows: it is a member of the genus Fabricia, which is a part of the tribe Goodenieae. This plant is closely related to other species within the family Goodeniaceae, such as Scaevola and Goodenia. Fabricia hamosa is further classified as a species within the kingdom Plantae.

parts: Fabricia hamosa is commonly known as the 'hooked leaf' or 'hooked caltrop', and it has been reported to have medicinal properties.
